description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and angular profile, featuring a mix of rough and pitted surfaces. The coloration transitions from a dark brownish top and sides to a lighter, almost chalky beige on the bottom and inner faces, suggesting weathering and mineral variation. The rock's surface is highly textured, with visible crevices, small protrusions, and a generally jagged appearance. Its mass and density are apparent from the solid, blocky form, and the lack of any visible fractures or major cracks. The rock appears naturally formed, with no evidence of human modification or artificial shaping. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, angular rock with dark brown and beige coloration, featuring rough, pitted surfaces and a solid, blocky form. Its natural weathering and mineral variation make it visually striking and suitable as an urban obstacle or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, highlighting the transition from dark brown on top to a lighter, textured beige on the lower portion.
description_view_1: The rock appears more triangular and upright, with the dark brown surface dominating and a sharp edge visible along the left side.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a concave, pitted surface with a pronounced beige coloration, emphasizing the rock's rough texture and mineral layering.
description_view_3: The rock is seen from a narrower angle, showing a mix of dark brown and lighter patches, with a prominent ridge running vertically.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
